name: Nara Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity, harmony and the timeless heritage of the ancient capital
deep red, suggesting the historic vermilion lacquer of Nara's temples and the rising sun

description: A white field charged at the centre with a deep-red disc stylising the katakana ナ of the prefecture's name.
history: Nara's prefectural symbol was adopted on 14 February 1968. The flag, with the maroon emblem on a white field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: purity and peace, fitting the ancient capital and cradle of Japanese civilisation
a deep-red disc charged with a stylised katakana ナ (na) of 奈良 (Nara); the circular form represents harmony and the long-uninterrupted history of the prefecture as the ancient capital of Japan.
